title: The large-read guard: deny an unbounded Read of a big file, name both escapes
action: Per D1/D2/D3/D4. Extend the EXISTING READ_TOOLS branch in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs (guards.checkRead) with a size check. Do NOT add a hooks/catalog.mjs entry and do NOT regenerate hooks/hooks.json, hooks/claude-hooks.json or .codex/hooks.json — the PreToolUse matcher already covers Read and routes it here, so a new catalog entry would be a second hook on the same event for no reason. DENY (exit 2 + stderr, the only verdict that reaches the model on PreToolUse — adapter.mjs ADVISORY_EVENTS is PreCompact|SubagentStop|Stop only, so there is no warn verdict to use) when ALL of: tool is Read; the resolved path is a regular file inside the worktree; its line count is >= the threshold; and NEITHER offset NOR limit is present in the tool input. Threshold: read from .bee/config.json (add a `guards.max_read_lines` key, default 800 when absent, following the hookEnabled `!== false` precedent in .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs:1339 for absent-key defaults). The denial message MUST name the file, its line count, the threshold, and BOTH escapes verbatim: pass `limit` (and optionally `offset`) to read a slice, or dispatch a `bee-extract` worker for the whole file. Counting lines: read the file once and count newlines; guard against binary and huge files by capping the read, and never let a stat/read error deny — any error path returns allow (fail-open, matching this hook's existing crash-catch posture at the bottom of main()). Add rows to hooks/test_write_guard.mjs covering: over-threshold + no offset/limit -> deny with both escapes present in stderr; over-threshold + limit -> allow; over-threshold + offset -> allow; under-threshold + no limit -> allow; a directory path -> allow; a nonexistent path -> allow; hooks.write-guard=false -> allow; and a custom guards.max_read_lines honoured. Then the D8 regen chain: `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write` (hooks/** is hashed at release_manifest.mjs:133) and re-run self-onboard so .bee/bin/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs mirrors the source.

truths: A Read of a >=800-line file with no offset and no limit is denied, and the stderr names the file, its line count, the threshold, and both escapes.
The same Read with a limit (or an offset) is allowed.
A Read of a file under the threshold is allowed with no offset or limit.
Every error path (missing file, directory, unreadable, binary) allows rather than denies.
Setting hooks.write-guard=false disables the size check along with the rest of the guard.
guards.max_read_lines in .bee/config.json overrides the 800 default; an absent key means 800.
.bee/bin/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs is byte-identical to hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs after the regen chain.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: The size branch in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, new rows in hooks/test_write_guard.mjs, the guards.max_read_lines key, refreshed manifest and mirror
prohibitions: Never add a hooks/catalog.mjs entry or regenerate the three hook projections — the existing matcher already covers Read.
Never deny on an error path; a guard that cannot read the file must allow.
Never block a Read that carries offset or limit, at any size.

verify: node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Extended the existing READ_TOOLS branch of hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs with a size check: a Read of a file at or above guards.max_read_lines (default 800, from .bee/config.json) carrying neither offset nor limit is DENIED with exit 2, and the stderr names the file, its line count, the threshold, and both escapes (pass limit, or dispatch a bee-extract worker). No hooks/catalog.mjs entry and no projection regen — the PreToolUse matcher already routes Read here. Fail-open on every error path: non-file, over-25MB, binary sniff, or any thrown stat/read returns allow. Regen chain ran (release_manifest --write, self-onboard); .bee/bin/hooks mirror byte-identical. Two facts corrected at implementation time and recorded as CONTEXT D10: the harness already caps a Read at 25k tokens with a pagination instruction, so the guard's real value is the 800-to-1100-line band plus converting a silent partial read into an explicit worker route, NOT top-end context protection; and the guard cannot be dogfooded from this session because the executing hook resolves to the main checkout's copy, which lacks the change until merge (grep -c: 1 in worktree, 0 in main).
